evidence_basis_summary: Neither source concerns what materials belong in an analysis universe; both treat drafting/citation behavior at the manuscript level.
notes: Upgraded low to high: full text clearly confirms role is out of scope for this case.

evidence_basis_summary: Neither full-text source engages causal/mechanism claims at stake; Science paper is descriptive about LLM adoption effects and CHI paper is about writing process.
notes: Upgraded low to high: direct full-text check confirms role not applicable.

evidence_basis_summary: Full texts do not turn on category boundary setting; C029_S1 uses a 20-topic codebook but this is instrumental measurement not a construct boundary contested in the case.
notes: Upgraded low to medium: sources touch only instrumentally on codebook building.

evidence_basis_summary: C029_P1 shows LLM adopters cite books and younger less-cited works more (Fig. 4) so AI broadens the evidence base; human role in keeping corpus plural is implicitly reshaped but not directly theorized.
notes: Upgrade from low to medium and flip observable from no to partial: Science paper directly documents citation-base diversification under LLM use; status set to conditional because human pluralist work is not centrally discussed.

evidence_basis_summary: C029_P1 argues journals/funders/tenure committees must evolve evaluation and disclosure frameworks and flags the AI detection/attribution limitation (SM S5.6); C029_S1 designs a logging/replay platform (AI-CRP) capturing keystrokes and suggestion traces for accountability.
notes: Upgraded low to high: both sources directly motivate protocol/provenance design and logging.

evidence_basis_summary: Neither source pressures transporting a model-generated result across populations or designs; C029_P1 notes non-causal identification but does not invoke counterfactual transport.
notes: Upgraded low to high: direct confirmation role is out of scope.

evidence_basis_summary: C029_S1 relies on cue-based retrospective interviews with 19 participants where interviewers interpret writing traces in situ; contextual human interpretation of replays is central to RQ1 findings.
notes: Upgrade from low to medium and flip observable from no to partial: CHI methods section directly evidences an interviewer/interpreter role; not the primary focus of Case 29 framing.

evidence_basis_summary: C029_P1 explicitly argues evaluation by journals/funders/tenure committees must change as language complexity loses signaling power and reviewers/editors may lean on pedigree; limitations note inability to identify which coauthor used the LLM bearing on authorship disclosure.
notes: Upgraded low to high: Science paper centrally treats allocation of quality signals and evaluative labor across scientific institutions.

evidence_basis_summary: No contested category-definition work is central in either source beyond the CHI team's instrumental 20-topic scheme.
notes: Upgraded low to medium via direct full-text check.

evidence_basis_summary: C029_S1 documents how opinionated AI suggestions collapse perspective pluralism (writers drift toward AI-seeded framings) and argues for preserving writer agency over multiple viewpoints.
notes: Upgrade from low to medium and flip observable from no to partial: reactive writing finding directly implicates perspective preservation, though framed as opinion-shift rather than sampling design.

evidence_basis_summary: Neither source engages counterfactual transport of findings.
notes: Upgraded low to high based on direct full-text confirmation.

evidence_basis_summary: Neither source turns on benchmarking or simulator calibration; C029_P1 validates an AI-detection classifier but does not frame it as a calibration-architect case.
notes: Upgraded low to high: direct confirmation role is out of scope.

evidence_basis_summary: C029_S1 interviewers use replay-anchored verbal protocols that shape what participants disclose about their writing process.
notes: Upgrade from low to medium and flip observable from no to partial based on CHI methods detail.

evidence_basis_summary: C029_S1 researchers interpret time-stamped interaction traces and writing replays as contextual evidence that sole transcript reading could not deliver.
notes: Upgrade from low to medium and flip observable from no to partial via CHI method description.

evidence_basis_summary: Neither source is about title/abstract triage; C029_P1 discusses reviewer heuristics becoming uninformative which is tangentially adjacent but not a first-pass screening workflow.
notes: Upgraded low to medium: direct full-text check rules out a screener workflow.

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: No ground-truth-rich labeling task is central; C029_S1 uses GPT models for topic assignment as an auxiliary measurement step but not as a case-level coding task under study.
notes: Upgraded low to medium via direct full-text check.

evidence_basis_summary: C029_P1 shows LLM-assisted manuscripts have systematically higher writing complexity and that polished prose is decoupling from substantive merit; C029_S1 documents AI assuming sentence-level drafting while writers shift to evaluation.
notes: Upgraded low to high: both sources provide direct full-text evidence of sentence/paragraph drafting being absorbed by AI.
